text:
Traffic forecasting serves as a cornerstone of multivariate time series analysis and remains a focal point of research within the AI community. To effectively capture the complex spatio-temporal heterogeneity and non-stationarity inherent in traffic streams, we introduce Spatio-Temporal Meta-Graph Learning, a novel graph structure learning framework designed specifically for spatio-temporal data. We instantiate this approach through the Meta-Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (MegaCRN), which integrates a Meta-Graph Learner—supported by a specialized Meta-Node Bank—directly into a GCRN encoder-decoder architecture. 

Our framework underwent rigorous evaluation across two standard benchmarks, METR-LA and PEMS-BAY, as well as a newly introduced large-scale dataset, EXPY-TKY, which encompasses 1,843 expressway links in Tokyo. Experimental results demonstrate that MegaCRN consistently outperforms current state-of-the-art models across all three datasets. Furthermore, qualitative analysis reveals that our model successfully disentangles distinct patterns among road links and time intervals, exhibiting robust adaptability to anomalous traffic conditions. The code and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.
